result:
Done. I implemented the three missing operations in `CsvRow.cs` (`Src.csproj` and `NeedsQuoting` untouched), and the project builds cleanly with no warnings.

- `EscapeField` returns the field verbatim unless `NeedsQuoting` holds, in which case it doubles internal quotes and wraps the result in double-quotes.
- `JoinRow` escapes each field and joins with commas; the empty list yields the empty string.
- `SplitRow` walks the line field by field: a field starting with `"` is scanned to its closing un-doubled quote (with `""` contributing one literal quote, and commas/CR/LF inside treated as content); otherwise the field runs to the next comma or end of line. The empty line parses to `[""]`, and a trailing comma produces a trailing empty field.

I verified behavior with a throwaway console project (deleted afterward, outside the source tree): round-trips of plain fields, empty fields, fields with commas/quotes/CRLF, quote-only fields, and leading/trailing empty fields all reproduced their inputs exactly, `JoinRow([])` returned `""`, and `SplitRow("")` returned a single empty field — all matching the spec, used 2 of the 10 build cycles.
